report_name: Massive Phishing Blitz Targets 2026 FIFA World Cup Fans with 79+ Fake Sites

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: A large-scale phishing campaign was conducted, targeting a global audience of football fans with at least 79 fraudulent websites that cloned the official FIFA site to steal credentials and payment information.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Attackers used paid search engine ads and social media posts containing links to direct potential victims to fraudulent FIFA websites.

attack_patterns.name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location
attack_patterns.description: Attackers registered dozens of malicious domains using typosquatting (e.g., vww-fifa.com) and lookalike keywords (e.g., fifa.sale) to impersonate the official FIFA website.

campaigns.name: 2026 FIFA World Cup Phishing Campaign
campaigns.description: A large-scale phishing operation targeting football fans worldwide with at least 79 fraudulent websites designed to impersonate the official FIFA website for financial fraud.
campaigns.objective: Steal user credentials, payment card details, and money through fake ticket and merchandise sales.

identities.name: FIFA
identities.description: The official football governing body whose website was impersonated by attackers to scam fans of the 2026 World Cup.

infrastructure.name: FIFA World Cup 2026 Phishing Websites
infrastructure.description: A network of at least 79 fraudulent websites that are clones of the official FIFA site, used to steal credentials and payment information through fake ticket and merchandise sales.

indicators.name: FIFA World Cup 2026 Phishing Domains
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Phishing Domain DNS Query
id: 9c5b1a3d-8e7f-4b2c-9d1a-6b8c4f0a1b2d
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to known malicious domains impersonating the official FIFA website for the 2026 World Cup.
references:
  - https://blog.knowbe4.com/phishing-attacks-target-2026-fifa-world-cup
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-15
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
  - attack.initial_access
falsepositives:
  - None expected.
level: high
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    QueryName:
      - 'vww-fifa.com'
      - 'fifa.sale'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries to known malicious domains impersonating the official FIFA website for the 2026 World Cup.

indicators.name: Potential FIFA Typosquatting or Lookalike Domain
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential FIFA Typosquatting or Lookalike Domain DNS Query
id: f8a1b2c3-d4e5-4f6a-b7c8-d9e0f1a2b3c4
status: experimental
description: Detects DNS queries to domains that may be typosquatting or lookalikes of the official FIFA domain, based on patterns observed in a large-scale phishing campaign.
references:
  - https://blog.knowbe4.com/phishing-attacks-target-2026-fifa-world-cup
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-15
tags:
  - attack.t1036.005
  - attack.resource_development
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate domains that contain 'fifa'.
level: medium
logsource:
  category: dns_query
  product: windows
detection:
  selection_pattern:
    QueryName|contains:
      - '.fifa.'
      - 'fifa-'
  filter_legit:
    QueryName|endswith:
      - '.fifa.com'
  condition: selection_pattern and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects DNS queries to domains that may be typosquatting or lookalikes of the official FIFA domain, based on patterns observed in a large-scale phishing campaign.

relationships.target_name: Match Legitimate Resource Name or Location

relationships.source_name: Potential FIFA Typosquatting or Lookalike Domain
